briefdescription.para: Get Latencies from cached regions, every x seconds.

memberdef.argsstring: (Credentials &NewCredentialsRef, const Settings &NewSettingsRef, FAccelByteMessagingSystem &InMessagingSystemRef, const QosManagerWPtr QosManagerWeak, TSharedPtr< AccelByte::FApiClient, ESPMode::ThreadSafe > const &InApiClient=nullptr)
memberdef.name: Qos

memberdef.argsstring: (Credentials &NewCredentialsRef, const Settings &NewSettingsRef, FAccelByteMessagingSystem &InMessagingSystemRef, const QosManagerWPtr QosManagerWeak, FAccelBytePlatformPtr const &InAccelBytePlatform)
memberdef.name: Qos

memberdef.argsstring: (const THandler< TArray< TPair< FString, float > > > &OnSuccess, FErrorHandler const &OnError)
memberdef.name: GetServerLatencies

param.type: const THandler< TArray< TPair< FString, float > > > &

briefdescription.para: Check server latencies (ping) per-region, with optional polling.

listitem.para: Optional Pulling (default off), unless .ini polling Settings.QosLatencyPollIntervalSecs > 0.

Latencies will be cached, then optionally polled (using cached QoS Servers).
Set @ Settings ini (# seconds) via QosLatencyPollIntervalSecs.

QoS Servers will optionally be polled.
Set @ Settings ini (# seconds) via QosServerLatencyPollIntervalSecs.

parameterdescription.para: If polling, this will also call every ping update success.
If polling, this will also call every ping update error.

memberdef.argsstring: (const THandler< TArray< TPair< FString, float > > > &OnSuccess, FErrorHandler const &OnError)
memberdef.name: GetActiveServerLatencies

param.type: const THandler< TArray< TPair< FString, float > > > &

briefdescription.para: Check server latencies (ping) per-region that have status ACTIVE.

parameterdescription.para: If polling, this will also call every ping update success.
If polling, this will also call every ping update error.

briefdescription.para: Wrapper for RemoveFromTicker() for PollLatenciesHandle and PollServerLatenciesHandle.
Check whether latency polling schedulers are active.
cleanup Latencies Poller handler from Unreal Core Ticker

memberdef.argsstring: (const TArray< FAccelByteModelsQosServer > &QosServers, const THandler< TArray< TPair< FString, float > > > &OnSuccess, FErrorHandler const &OnError)
memberdef.name: PingRegionsSetLatencies

param.type: const THandler< TArray< TPair< FString, float > > > &

briefdescription.para: For each server, ping them and record add to Latency TArray.

listitem.para:
Then, ping each region and cache returned Latencies.
QosServerList
OnSuccess
OnError

memberdef.argsstring: (const bool bPingRegionsOnSuccess, const THandler< TArray< TPair< FString, float > > > &OnPingRegionsSuccess, FErrorHandler const &OnError)
memberdef.name: CallGetQosServers

param.type: const THandler< TArray< TPair< FString, float > > > &

briefdescription.para: Sets QosServers, using this cache for future calls.

listitem.para:
Optionally (default off), via Settings.QosServerLatencyPollIntervalSecs, a separate polling interval can be set to update QosServers for the regions, themselves.
Not to be confused with Settings.QosLatencyPollIntervalSecs (uses server cached regions).

parameterdescription.para: Upon success, we'll call PingRegionsSetLatencies() from the results.

briefdescription.para: Poll every x second to refresh Latencies (pings) with cached (via QosServers) regions.

listitem.para: Only polls when QosLatencyPollIntervalSecs polling Settings > 0.
Explicitly calling this a 2nd+ time will reset the poller without conflict and use the new tick interval.
Not to be confused with InitGetServerLatenciesScheduler(), that caches the target server regions for Latencies polling.

parameterdescription.para: the interval for polling the latency in seconds

briefdescription.para: Resolves QOS server IP using the platform's socket subsystem.

parameterdescription.para: QOS server structure that will be modified

simplesect.para: true if resolution was successful, false otherwise

briefdescription.para: Send cached latencies to messaging system to notify the latencies is updated.
Handler for receiving lobby connected message from messaging system.
Qos to manage specific QoS (Latencies/Ping) Server(s).
